sglang.title: [Apple Silicon] Add custom Metal RoPE kernel with fused KV cache store
sglang.author: adityavaid
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This PR adds the first AOT-compiled Metal kernel to SGLang's Apple Silicon backend: a fused NeoX RoPE + KV pool scatter that replaces two mx.fast.rope calls and the per-request KV-pool scatter in batched decode with a single C++ entry that emits one Metal command buffer.
On Apple Silicon,

sglang.title: [AMD][CI] Update v4 CI setting and move the task to main branch

sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Previously, the DeepSeek-V4 model code on AMD GPUs lived on the amd/deepseek_v4 branch and had not been merged into main, so all V4 nightly CI jobs had to pull a dedicated rocm/sgl-dev:*-DSv4 image built from that branch.
After #26383, the DSv4 model + ROCm optimizations are now on main,

avoiding the AMD MI355 TPOT regression while leaving other backends unchanged.

publish_ready in the resolve_seq_lens_cpu path caused a TPOT perfo

attn_bmm_output = attn_bmm_output.transpose(0, 1).flatten(1, 2)

Kimi K2.5 needs these wrapper forwards for RL weight updates. RL weight sync code operates on the top-level model wrapper, but the actual language-model weight loading metadata lives on the inner language model. Without forwarding stacked_params_mapping, expert_params_mapping, mutate_weig

sglang.title: [attention] Fallback to Triton merge_state when FlashInfer hits CUDA thread limit
sglang.author: brucechanglongxu
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
FlashInfer's MergeState CUDA kernel launches with blockDim = (head_dim/vec_size, num_heads). When num_heads is large (e.g. with DP attention where attention_tp_size=1, putting all KV heads on a single rank), the total threads per block can exceed CUDA's limit of 1024 and the launch fails

Motivation

The current implementation for pinned memory with LoRA has two inefficiencies:

The entire weight is pinned on each rank instead of just the relevant slice.
All weights are pinned

sglang.author: ByronHsu
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
TLDR
The server crashed when the reqs in a batch have a mix of return_routed_experts = True and False. The same issue applies to output_hidden_states and indexer_topk. The PR follows the similar path as logprob to fix it, add a global return_routed_experts on the batch level, and append None if batc

sglang.title: Drop dead ScheduleBatch return_routed_experts/return_indexer_topk fields
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
These two ScheduleBatch dataclass fields are computed in init_new via any(req...) but never read at the batch level. The actual capture path is gated by the global server arg + per-req flags, so the batch aggregates are dead since introduction. Removing them.

The D2H copy of indptr[-1].item() needing synchronization

Reduce the time to merge for those specific PRs
Reduce resource contention for PRs that need the docs built

vllm.title: [Metrics] Exclude KV transfer tokens from iteration_tokens_total
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The iteration_tokens_total histogram was using IterationStats.num_prompt_tokens which includes all prompt token sources (local_compute + local_cache_hit + external_kv_transfer).
When using P/D, external_kv_transfer tokens inflated this metric by the full prompt length of each newly-arrived request,

vllm.title: [MoE Refactor] Remove supports_expert_map
vllm.author: bnellnm
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Remove supports_expert_map.  Not all experts classes support expert_maps but the ones that don't can simply ignore the map if it is passed.  This function was used by the cutlass experts to avoid passing the expert_map at runtime but the cutlass experts should be able to just ignore the map

vllm.title: [CI] Fix smoke test step key to bypass block gate
vllm.author: khluu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Renames the non-root smoke test step key from image-smoke-test to image-build-smoke-test
The pipeline-generator's _step_should_run() auto-runs steps whose key starts with "image-build" — the previous key didn't match, causing a manual block step to be inserted

Follow-up fix for #43712.

vllm.title: [PERF]MiniMax-M2 gate kernel
vllm.author: jeejeelee
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The MoE gate of MiniMax-M2 needs to perform GEMM in FP32 and requires converting the input to FP32, see router_logits. Therefore, in low-concurrency scenarios, up to 3 kernels may be launched in the worst case:

the first one is BF16-to-FP32 conversion
and the second and third are the two ke

vllm.author: gagandhakrey

Fix BaseRenderer.render_prompts_async which was a fake-async wrapper that called the synchronous render_prompts — blocking the asyncio event loop on pybase64.b64decode + torch.load + to_dense + dtype-cast work inside safe_load_prompt_embeds.

vllm.title: [ROCm] cmake: support PYTORCH_FOUND_HIP for torch 2.13 native HIP language support
vllm.author: nemanjaudovic
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
torch 2.13 switched from FindHIP.cmake (legacy) to CMake native enable_language(HIP) in commit pytorch/pytorch@d921fd0. The new approach sets PYTORCH_FOUND_HIP but never sets HIP_FOUND, which caused vllm's CMakeLists.txt to fall through to the fatal error path.
Fix the HIP detection condition in vll

vllm.title: [BugFix][Platform] Fix import vllm.platforms.rocm error on non-CUDA test_gpt_oss.py
vllm.author: Liangliang-Ma
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
vllm/platforms/rocm.py calls torch.cuda.get_device_properties("cuda").gcnArchName
at import time. On XPU/CPU torch builds this raises
AssertionError: Torch not compiled with CUDA enabled, breaking any
import vllm.platforms.rocm — e.g. pytest collection of
tests/models/quantization/test_gpt_oss.py (w

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix RMSNorm kernels to multiply in weight's native dtype
vllm.author: liulanze
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix a regression introduced by #40860 (DeepSeek V4 support) where csrc/layernorm_kernels.cu was changed to always upcast the RMSNorm weight to float32 before the final multiplication. The correct behaviour, per the Python spec in vllm/ir/ops/layernorm.py:20, is to multiply in the weight's na
